title: TLS Certificate Rotation Drift Incident-Response Runbook
task_scope: security operations / TLS certificate-rotation drift containment
operational_purpose: reconcile certificate-rotation drift evidence, approved attenuation windows, and stateful environment risk into a deterministic containment queue

workflow_repair.token_check_scope: executable code only (exclude comments and docstrings)

digest_payloads.trust_path_digest: first 12 lowercase hex chars of sha256(origin|trust_exposure_score|semicolon_joined_target_rows), where each target row in trust_reachable_envs order is target:score:path_nodes_joined_by_>

field_types._note: Declares the literal vocabulary and JSON container type of fields whose spelling or shape the verifier matches exactly. These are output-format facts only; the governing rules that decide which value a given window receives live in the governance review log.

priority.note: Exactly these three lowercase labels. Alternative schemes (P0/P1/P2, sev tiers, numeric ranks) are rejected. Which tier a queued window earns is governed by the log's priority decision.

trust_reachable_envs.note: A JSON array of environment-name strings, never an object/mapping keyed by env. Which envs are reachable, and the path retention behind them, are governed by the log's trust decisions.

trust_strongest_path.note: A JSON array of environment names giving the full node sequence, origin first.

muted.coercion: booleans unchanged; strings true/1/yes (after str(...).strip().lower()) -> true, all other strings -> false; any non-string non-boolean value -> Python bool(value)

severity_counts.note: Always enumerates all four levels in this order, emitting 0 for levels with no canonical alerts.

response_queue.json_format: compact JSON lines via json.dumps(..., separators=(',', ':'))

summary_json.severity_counts_contract: severity_counts carries one entry per severity in severity_counts_key_order, in that order, including severities with no canonical alerts; those are emitted with a count of 0 rather than omitted
summary_json.total_unmuted_duration_ms_contract: sum of merged window duration_ms values from drift_windows.json (not raw alert durations)
summary_json.total_ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms_cont: sum ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms over all drift windows, including windows not admitted to the response queue

maximum_aggregation_domains.queue_only: max_stability_pressure_score, max_volatility_index, max_defer_pressure_score, max_ledger_pressure_score, max_trust_exposure_score, and max_stability_index are maxima over final admitted response_queue rows only; use 0 when the queue is empty
maximum_aggregation_domains.all_drift_windows: max_carry_out_ms is the maximum over every drift window, including windows not admitted to the response queue; use 0 when there are no windows

checksum_serialization_contract.trailing_delimiter: none: do not append a newline or pipe after the final row
checksum_serialization_contract.muted_encoding: integer text 1 when normalized muted is true, otherwise integer text 0; never serialize JSON true/false
checksum_serialization_contract.canonical_alert_rows: one alert_id|env|start_ms|end_ms|severity|muted_int|signature row for each canonical deduplicated alert, sorted by (env ascending, start_ms ascending, alert_id ascending)
checksum_serialization_contract.freeze_rows: one env|start_ms|end_ms row for each compacted interval, sorted by env ascending and then interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending
checksum_serialization_contract.scoped_rows: one env|severity_scope|start_ms|end_ms row for reopen/rotation/defer using only compacted rows whose severity_scope is in that source's scope_values allowlist; sort by (env,severity_scope) ascending and then interval (start_ms,end_ms) ascending
checksum_serialization_contract.trust_edge_rows: one source_env|target_env|weight row per canonical deduplicated directed edge, sorted by source_env then target_env ascending
checksum_serialization_contract.ledger_rows: one env|start_ms|idle_gap_ms|carry_in_ms|carry_out_ms|ledger_adjusted_actionable_ms row for every drift window, sorted by env ascending and start_ms ascending

summary_json.reopen_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of checksum_serialization_contract scoped_rows built from compacted reopen intervals
summary_json.rotation_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of checksum_serialization_contract scoped_rows built from compacted rotation intervals
summary_json.defer_compaction_checksum: SHA-256 of checksum_serialization_contract scoped_rows built from compacted defer intervals
summary_json.queue_hash_checksum: sha256('|'.join(queue_hash values in final queue order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.window_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(window_digest values in final queue order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.trust_path_digest_checksum: sha256('|'.join(trust_path_digest values in final queue order)) lowercase 64 hex chars
summary_json.schema_version_note: Emit summary.json schema_version exactly as this summary_json.schema_version value ('cert-rotation-v1'). Do NOT use the report_spec top-level 'version' field (2.0), which is the contract document version, not the output schema version.

drift_windows_json.field_policy: Each window row carries EXACTLY the required_fields keys — no extras and no omissions. Queue-level fields (ticket_id, priority, queue_hash, window_digest, stability_pressure_score, volatility_index, defer_pressure_score, ledger_pressure_score, stability_index) belong only to response_queue.jsonl rows. env is the top-level map key, not a row field.
drift_windows_json.source_alert_ids_order: `source_alert_ids` is a JSON array of the canonical alert_id strings that formed the window, sorted ASCENDING by string comparison. This ordering applies in both drift_windows.json and response_queue.jsonl. The comma-joined form used inside signature and checksum payloads is built from that same ascending order.

semantics_source.notes: This document is the output contract only: file paths, schemas, required fields, repair tokens, digest payloads and checksum serialization. It does NOT define how values are derived.
semantics_source.authority: Canonicalization, dedupe tie-breaks, window merging, the freeze/reopen/rotation/defer attenuation layers and their scope rules, probe scoring, the risk ledger, certificate chain trust traversal, queue admission, priority rules and queue ordering were settled by the rotation governance board; the authoritative record is /app/incident/rotation_review_log.md. Where an early draft proposal conflicts with a later governance decision, the later decision wins.

governing_entry_index._note: Navigation aid only. It records WHICH review entries carry the governing rule for each pipeline stage; it does not restate any rule, value, divisor, threshold or rounding direction. Entries not listed are superseded by a later dated entry or carry no reconciler semantics.
governing_entry_index.rounding_warning: Rounding direction is not uniform across the pipeline and is not inferable from any neighbouring divisor, including between the two halves of the same probe score. Each divisor's direction is fixed by its own entry; #CR-5364 records the map.
governing_entry_index.reversal_warning: At least one stage has had the DIRECTION of a comparison reversed by a later entry, not merely its constants retuned. Check each stage's latest dated entry before assuming an earlier entry's ordering still holds.

by_output_field._note: Field-level navigation. For each output field, the pipeline STAGES whose review entries govern how its value is derived. This narrows where to read; it does not identify which entry in a stage is the latest, and it states no formula, divisor, threshold or rounding direction. Read the stage's entries in the log and apply the latest dated decision for each rule.
by_output_field.aggregate_note: Each `total_*` duration is the sum over drift windows of the per-window field of the same name minus its `total_` prefix, so it is governed by whichever entry governs that per-window field. One is easy to misread and is called out in the contract: see `summary_json.total_unmuted_duration_ms_contract` for which quantity it sums.
by_output_field.constant_note: `schema_version` is a fixed contract constant, not a derived value, and is not governed by any review entry. It is recorded in this contract.
